items.title: How some Quebec companies are making the leap towards digital sovereignty
items.summary_auto: More and more Quebec companies want to offer alternatives to U.S. tech giants. With increasing geopolitical tensions, some believe the time is right for Quebecers to give digital sovereignty a chance.

items.title: Junos 2026: All the weekend's winners
items.summary_auto: The second night of the 55th annual Juno Awards kicked off Sunday with a performance by Rush, after a majority of the roughly 50 categories were awarded at a banquet on Saturday.

items.title: Mogul skiing star Mikaël Kingsbury closes out career with final victory on home turf
items.summary_auto: Mikaël Kingsbury’s last run in competition took place on his home mountain of Saint-Sauveur, Que. The moguls star might be retiring from the sport but for some he is a living legend who has inspired a new generation of freestyle skiers.

items.title: Junos 2026: bbno$ wins fan choice, while the Beaches, Rush make history at Juno Awards
Must-see looks from the 2026 Juno Awards carpet
items.summary_auto: Nelly Furtado, Raf-Saperra, Tia Wood and more make bold style statements.

items.title: P.E.I. cabinet minister's removal tied to allegations around $100K donation made by private developer
items.summary_auto: Premier Rob Lantz removed a senior member of his inner circle over a legal opinion detailing how a $100,000 donation from a private developer was allegedly made to a group in his district.

items.title: Canada's Josh Liendo wins record-tying 4th straight NCAA swimming title in 100-yard freestyle
items.summary_auto: Canadian Olympian Josh Liendo capped an historic week of swimming at the NCAA championships on Saturday in Atlanta, posting a wire-to-wire victory in the 100-yard freestyle for a record-tying fourth consecutive title in the event and ninth overall.

items.title: Sask. and federal NDP clash over stance on fossil fuels in open letter
items.summary_auto: Saskatchewan NDP Leader Carla Beck has slammed newly-elected federal NDP leader Avi Lewis over his stance on fossil fuels projects.

items.title: European Parliament member Rima Hassan says she was denied entry to Canada
items.summary_auto: A French-Palestinian member of the European Parliament says she was denied entry into Canada only days before she was scheduled to participate in conferences in Montreal. Rima Hassan wrote online that she was "prevented" from coming to Canada in what she described as an attempt at censorship.

items.title: Pro-Palestine European Parliament member Rima Hassan says she was denied entry to Canada
items.summary_auto: A French-Palestinian member of the European Parliament says she was denied entry into Canada only days before she was scheduled to participate in conferences in Montreal. Rima Hassan wrote online that she was "prevented" from coming to Canada in what she described as an attempt at censorship.

items.title: Lethal bat disease detected in Alberta's Rocky Mountains for first time
items.summary_auto: White-nose syndrome, a deadly disease that has decimated bat populations across North America in recent years, has been detected in the Rocky Mountains of Alberta for the first time, marking a significant spread since it was first confirmed to be in the province two years ago.

items.title: 'The crew is ready': Calgary-born astronaut to be voice link to Artemis II moon mission
items.summary_auto: Jenni Gibbons was always drawn to the stars. Now, the Calgary-born astronaut is set to take part in NASA's Artemis II mission, which will send humans to the moon for the first time in more than 50 years.

items.title: North Korea conducts engine test for missile capable of targeting U.S.
items.summary_auto: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un observed a test of a high-thrust, solid-fuel engine and hailed it as a development to boost the country's strategic military capability, state media reported Sunday.

items.title: Sask. movie theatres to set their own film warnings as province scraps age-based ratings
items.summary_auto: The province's Film Content Information Act, taking effect April 1, ends mandatory government approval. Each theatre will be required to write and display their own content description instead of the familiar age-based ratings.

items.title: Oxford Frozen Foods fined $10K for mislabelling blueberries as Canadian
items.summary_auto: Nova Scotia-based Oxford Frozen Foods has been fined $10,000 for mislabelling its blueberries as Canadian at a time when more consumers are looking to buy local in grocery stores.

items.title: I've lived in 3 provinces. Canada's health care is anything but universal
items.summary_auto: Shannon Flaman has lived in Saskatchewan, B.C. and Alberta. Her experiences in emergency rooms and finding family doctors have changed the way she sees the country's health care.

items.title: Ottawa's big bet on world's largest cricket farm ran into a simple problem: the 'yuck factor'
items.summary_auto: Ottawa backed the world's largest cricket farm with tens of millions in public money. It collapsed before reaching full production, exposing a gap between hype and demand while also leaving questions about how much of that money was ever recovered.

items.title: 'You are going to be outraged': Mother speaks ahead of inquest into daughter's death at Niagara hospital
items.summary_auto: Community members gathered Thursday in Fort Erie, Ont., to pay tribute to Heather Winterstein, a young woman whose death raised questions about how Indigenous people and those with mental health and substance use issues are treated in the health-care system. An inquest into her death begins Monday.

items.title: Amid Iran conflict, combative neighbours Armenia and Azerbaijan try to stay above the fray
items.summary_auto: Armenia and Azerbaijan, both of which border Iran to its north, have seen their fair share of conflict. The current war around Iran has drawn in Arab Gulf states — and these two neighbours may not be able to avoid becoming involved, too.

items.title: Air traffic controller’s 'I messed up' highlights human limits — but experts urge caution
items.summary_auto: The comment, captured on live air traffic control audio, has quickly become one of the most haunting elements of the crash that killed two Canadian pilots and injured dozens more. But aviation experts warn the remark risks oversimplifying what is potentially a more complex chain of failures.

items.title: How AI is infiltrating the dating world, from crafting flirty messages to matchmaking
items.summary_auto: Companies have been keen to jump on the AI bandwagon in the last year, unveiling new AI features that promise to help users level up their dating game. But some users, particularly women, say it's making it more difficult to tell fact from fiction, and making the quest for love more difficult.
